title: Agent Infrastructure Consolidation
scope: All 9 active Receptor ecosystem repositories (.agents/.agent directories)

findings.description: Each of the 9 repos maintains its own .agents/ directory with no symlink architecture. Skill updates in dev-environment never propagate automatically; local copies drift silently.

tasks.description: Create the 4 category subdirectories in dev-environment/.agents/: frontend-nextjs/, supabase-infrastructure/, backend-python/, docusaurus/ — each with workflows/ and rules/ subdirs.

Write canonical workflow files (audit-workflow.md, implement-audit-workflow.md, finalise-local-audit.md) into each of the 4 category directories, generalising repo-specific rows. Also write frontend-nextjs/rules/git.md using the ecosystem-wide pre-commit bypass rule (identical content from planner-frontend/rules/pre-commit.md, preference-frontend/rules/git.md, and workforce-frontend/rules/git.md).
Delete local .agents/ workflow and rules contents from all 9 repos (keep directory for symlink step).

Create symlinks: planner-frontend/.agents -> frontend-nextjs, preference-frontend/.agents -> frontend-nextjs, workforce-frontend/.agents -> frontend-nextjs, website-frontend/.agents -> frontend-nextjs, supabase-receptor/.agents -> supabase-infrastructure, match-backend/.agents -> backend-python, receptor-planner/.agents -> backend-python, common-bond/.agents -> docusaurus.

Add .agents to .gitignore in each of the 9 child repos. CONFIRMED: zero of 8 audited repos currently have .agents in .gitignore (rotator_worker not checked as it has no .agents). This task applies to all 9 repos.

findings.description: dev-environment/.agents/ has no category-level organisation. Workflow files with category-specific context live in individual repos rather than in a shared canonical location.

tasks.description: Addressed by ARCH-01-T1 and ARCH-01-T2 — no separate tasks required.

findings.description: planner-frontend has local copies of 9 skills (adversarial-code-review was previously committed as deleted; remaining: design-md, enhance-prompt, find-skills, nextjs-app-router-patterns, playwright-best-practices, playwright-skill, react-components, stitch-loop, vitest, vitest-failure-investigation). All exist in dev-environment. Approximately 89 files of pure duplication.

tasks.description: Delete frontend/planner-frontend/.agents/skills/ entirely (9 remaining skill directories). adversarial-code-review was already deleted and committed. Addressed by ARCH-01 symlink.

findings.description: planner-frontend has rules/vitest-failure-investigation.md (mirrors dev-environment skill) and rules/pre-commit.md. The vitest rule is the rule-as-skill anti-pattern.

tasks.description: Delete rules/vitest-failure-investigation.md. For pre-commit.md: content is identical to preference-frontend/rules/git.md and workforce-frontend/rules/git.md (ecosystem-wide 'don't bypass pre-commit hooks' rule). Promote this content to dev-environment/.agents/frontend-nextjs/rules/git.md (addressed by ARCH-01-T2), then delete the local pre-commit.md.

findings.description: preference-frontend had adversarial-code-review deleted from disk during this audit session, but the deletion was NEVER committed to git. git log on preference-frontend main shows no deletion commit. Additionally, .agents/skills/ still contains vitest-failure-investigation dir. Implementation agent must commit the deletion.

tasks.description: Commit the disk-level deletion of frontend/preference-frontend/.agents/skills/adversarial-code-review/ (directory was removed from disk but not staged/committed). Also commit removal of vitest-failure-investigation from skills/ (it belongs in workflows via required_skills, not as a local skills/ copy).

findings.description: preference-frontend has rules/vitest-failure-investigation.md and rules/git.md. The vitest rule mirrors the canonical dev-environment skill.

tasks.description: rules/git.md content is identical to planner-frontend/rules/pre-commit.md and workforce-frontend/rules/git.md. It is promoted to dev-environment/.agents/frontend-nextjs/rules/git.md (addressed by ARCH-01-T2). Delete local copies after symlink. Also delete rules/vitest-failure-investigation.md.

findings.description: workforce-frontend had adversarial-code-review deleted from disk during this audit session, but the deletion was NEVER committed to git. git log on workforce-frontend shows no deletion commit. .agents/skills/ still contains vitest-failure-investigation dir. Implementation agent must commit the deletion.

tasks.description: Commit the disk-level deletion of frontend/workforce-frontend/.agents/skills/adversarial-code-review/ (directory was removed from disk but not staged/committed). Also commit removal of vitest-failure-investigation from skills/.

findings.description: workforce-frontend has rules/vitest-failure-investigation.md and rules/git.md. The vitest rule mirrors the canonical dev-environment skill.

tasks.description: rules/git.md content is identical to planner-frontend/rules/pre-commit.md and preference-frontend/rules/git.md. Promoted to frontend-nextjs/rules/git.md (addressed by ARCH-01-T2). Delete local copies after symlink. Also delete rules/vitest-failure-investigation.md.

findings.description: supabase-receptor had adversarial-code-review deleted from disk during this audit session, but the deletion was NEVER committed to git. git log on supabase-receptor shows no deletion commit. .agents/skills/ is now an empty directory. Implementation agent must commit the deletion.

tasks.description: Commit the disk-level deletion of supabase-receptor/.agents/skills/adversarial-code-review/ (directory was removed from disk but not staged/committed).

findings.description: supabase-receptor has a rules file whose content mirrors the canonical dev-environment supabase-standards skill. This is the rule-as-skill anti-pattern.

tasks.description: Delete supabase-receptor/.agents/rules/supabase-standards.md. The canonical skill is referenced by absolute path in supabase-receptor's audit-workflow.md.

findings.description: supabase-postgres-best-practices (20+ reference files) exists only in common-bond's local .agents/skills/ and is not in dev-environment. Under the target architecture it should live in docusaurus/skills/ in dev-environment.

tasks.description: Copy documentation/common-bond/.agents/skills/supabase-postgres-best-practices/ into dev-environment/.agents/docusaurus/skills/. Update the reference in docusaurus/workflows/audit-workflow.md to the new canonical path.

findings.description: common-bond's audit-workflow.md (197 lines, dual-mode) contains Docusaurus-specific context not shared with any other documentation repo. Under the target architecture this becomes the canonical docusaurus/workflows/audit-workflow.md in dev-environment.

tasks.description: Use common-bond's audit-workflow.md as the basis for dev-environment/.agents/docusaurus/workflows/audit-workflow.md. Generalise any common-bond-specific references. Addressed as part of ARCH-01-T2.

findings.description: frontend/.agent/ exists at the monorepo parent level which is not a git repository. It contains 5 files: 4 workflow files (audit-workflow.md, finalise-local-audit.md, frontend-test-audit.md, implement-audit-workflow.md) and 1 rules file (component-sync.md). UNIQUE CONTENT: (a) frontend-test-audit.md is also present in dev-environment/.agents/workflows/ but may have diverged — verify and canonicalize. (b) component-sync.md (33 lines) is UNIQUE and VALUABLE — defines the design-first UI sync rule requiring all new components be implemented in design-frontend first. This must be promoted to dev-environment/.agents/frontend-nextjs/rules/component-sync.md, NOT deleted. Evidence: file contents read during iterative review.

tasks.description: DO NOT simply delete frontend/.agent/. Steps: (1) Compare frontend/.agent/workflows/frontend-test-audit.md with dev-environment/.agents/workflows/frontend-test-audit.md — merge any unique content. (2) Promote frontend/.agent/rules/component-sync.md to dev-environment/.agents/frontend-nextjs/rules/component-sync.md. (3) Remaining 3 workflow stubs (audit, implement, finalise) are duplicates — discard. (4) Delete frontend/.agent/ entirely.

findings.description: rotator_worker (Flutter/Dart) has no .agents/ directory. It is the only repository in the ecosystem with zero agent infrastructure. Modernisation of this legacy repo is planned. Evidence: find frontend/rotator_worker -name '.agents' returns no results. Recommend a new dart-flutter/ category in dev-environment/.agents/ rather than mapping to backend-python/.

tasks.description: Create dev-environment/.agents/dart-flutter/ category with workflows/ and rules/ subdirs. Write dart-flutter-specific audit-workflow.md, implement-audit-workflow.md, finalise-local-audit.md. Symlink frontend/rotator_worker/.agents -> dev-environment/.agents/dart-flutter/. Add .agents to rotator_worker .gitignore.

findings.description: dev-environment/.agents/ has no README.md or contribution guide. The directory contains schemas/, scripts/, skills/, and workflows/ but no documentation explaining the architecture, how to add a new skill, how to add a new category, or naming conventions. Implementation agents creating new category structures will lack a canonical reference and may make inconsistent decisions.

tasks.description: Create dev-environment/.agents/README.md documenting: (1) directory structure and purpose of each subdirectory, (2) how to add a new skill (naming, SKILL.md frontmatter, registration), (3) how to add a new category (dir structure, required workflow files, symlink convention), (4) naming conventions (snake-case skills, category dirs), (5) how the symlink architecture works.

findings.description: documentation/receptor-ecosystem does not exist as a local repository. When created, it will need to be mapped to the docusaurus category symlink.

tasks.description: When receptor-ecosystem repo is created: create symlink documentation/receptor-ecosystem/.agents -> dev-environment/.agents/docusaurus. Add .agents to its .gitignore.

findings.description: match-backend has local copies of python-design-patterns and python-testing-patterns, both of which exist in dev-environment/.agents/skills/. Discovered in iterative review — missed in initial audit. Evidence: find backend/match-backend/.agents/skills -type f returns SKILL.md for both.

tasks.description: Delete backend/match-backend/.agents/skills/python-design-patterns/ and backend/match-backend/.agents/skills/python-testing-patterns/. Addressed by the ARCH-01 symlink to backend-python/ category.

findings.description: receptor-planner has local copies of python-design-patterns and python-testing-patterns, both of which exist in dev-environment/.agents/skills/. Discovered in iterative review — missed in initial audit. Evidence: find backend/receptor-planner/.agents/skills -type f returns SKILL.md for both.

tasks.description: Delete backend/receptor-planner/.agents/skills/python-design-patterns/ and backend/receptor-planner/.agents/skills/python-testing-patterns/. Addressed by the ARCH-01 symlink to backend-python/ category.

findings.description: The symlink migration creates a window where some repos are symlinked and others are not. An agent invoked mid-migration will see inconsistent state. Mitigation: execute atomically per category (all frontend repos in one pass, then backend, then supabase, then docusaurus).

tasks.description: Implementation agent must process all repos in a single category before moving to the next. Recommended sequence: (1) frontend-nextjs: planner, preference, workforce, website, (2) backend-python: match-backend, receptor-planner, (3) supabase-infrastructure: supabase-receptor, (4) docusaurus: common-bond.

clarifications.decision: documentation/common-bond maps to the docusaurus category (not frontend-nextjs). A 4th category directory docusaurus/ is created in dev-environment to serve documentation repos.
frontend/ is not a git repository. The .agent/ directory is orphaned. Its useful content will be absorbed into frontend-nextjs/audit-workflow.md before deletion.

clarifications.item: Option A vs Option B symlink approach
clarifications.decision: Option A approved: full symlink of .agents/ to the appropriate category. Repo-specific context rows are generalised at category level; agents read actual repo files for specifics.

rules/vitest-failure-investigation.md files in planner, preference, and workforce frontends are the rule-as-skill anti-pattern. They will be deleted; the skill is referenced via absolute path in workflow required_skills.

All three instances (planner-frontend/rules/pre-commit.md, preference-frontend/rules/git.md, workforce-frontend/rules/git.md) are byte-for-byte identical ecosystem-wide content. Promote to dev-environment/.agents/frontend-nextjs/rules/git.md; delete local copies.

The adversarial-code-review skill was deleted from disk in preference-frontend, workforce-frontend, and supabase-receptor during the audit session but was never committed. PREF-01, WF-01, SR-01 remain open. The implementation agent must commit these deletions as its first step.

supabase-receptor/.agents/workflows/audit-workflow.md is fully JSON-refactor compliant and contains unique, valuable Repo Context rows (stack: PostgreSQL/Supabase/Deno, verify command: supabase db reset && supabase test db && deno check). These rows MUST be preserved in dev-environment/.agents/supabase-infrastructure/workflows/audit-workflow.md when creating the canonical category workflow.

implementationPhases.rationale: Create category structure, canonical workflows, and commit uncommitted deletion leftovers
Promote supabase-postgres-best-practices skill to docusaurus category
Delete local duplicates, create symlinks, update .gitignore

deferred.reason: documentation/receptor-ecosystem repository does not yet exist. This task applies when the repo is created.
